title: The Agent identifies that Acme Cloud Q1 2026 SaaS sales tax jurisdictions in scope are Texas, New York, Washington, and Arizona only (or equivalent state names or abbreviations).
justification: The prompt asks which jurisdictions belong in Q1. The memo-governed taxable-state list is four states: CA, GA, NC, FL, and IL are excluded at $0 for this quarter.
evidence: Per OE memo/Slack/GL steps: Hannah's correction (Slack 96dadaa7bd2b53b888044ccba442af4d), JE je_b5e31cb22eb246d5 business_justification, and memo doc_84905e8e14ad4d96 metadata all limit Q1 to TX, NY, WA, AZ. Check the Agent's final response to Ming.

title: The Agent identifies that the sales tax expense account on the posted FP-2026-03 accrual is 525000.
justification: The prompt asks which expense account carried the entry. The posted adjusting entry debits 525000.
evidence: Per OE GL pull: JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-03-0076 (je_b5e31cb22eb246d5) line 1 DR 525000 Sales Tax Expense for $42,180.55. Check the Agent's final response to Ming.

title: The Agent identifies that the credit side of the posted FP-2026-03 accrual is account 225000.
justification: The prompt asks which payable account carried the entry. Acme posts the liability to 225000 on this combined accrual.
evidence: Per OE GL pull: JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-03-0076 line 2 CR 225000 Accrued Salaries & Bonuses for $42,180.55. Check the Agent's final response to Ming.

title: The Agent reports the tax-prep channel and filing-support total for Q1 as $40,364.55.
justification: The prompt states the channel total and ledger diverge; the Agent must identify the channel-side figure before stating the gap.
evidence: Per OE Slack steps: Tom's grand-total post aae79bd6932c55328e9004cfdb07be7d and filing follow-up f577e2878d3757749e2ba08a3b0abf2f both tie four states to $40,364.55. Check the Agent's final response to Ming.

title: The Agent reports the posted FP-2026-03 ledger accrual amount as $42,180.55.
justification: The prompt requires reconciling the posted March entry against the channel discussion; the governing GL amount is $42,180.55.
evidence: Per OE GL pull via vault link doc_3e28a0ac0f464b56 to je_b5e31cb22eb246d5: posted JE total_debit and total_credit $42,180.55. Check the Agent's final response to Ming.

title: The Agent states the gap between the channel total and the posted ledger accrual as $1,816.00 (or similar).
justification: The prompt explicitly asks the Agent to state the gap between the diverging figures.
evidence: Derived from universe GT: $42,180.55 minus $40,364.55 equals $1,816.00. Check the Agent's final response to Ming.

title: The Agent names $42,180.55 as the figure Ming should sign off on for Acme Q1 SaaS sales tax partner clearance, per the posted closed-period adjusting entry that governs (not the channel/receipts-supported total of $40,364.55).
justification: The prompt asks which number governs and which figure to sign off on, and explicitly states the March period is closed and the entry as posted is what governs. Agents who treat the channel/receipts-supported total as controlling fail this discriminating rubric.
evidence: Per OE GL step: posted adjusting entry in closed acme_cloud_FP-2026-03 controls partner clearance per the prompt's 'entry as posted is what governs' anchor, not the Slack grand total of $40,364.55. Check the Agent's final response to Ming.

title: The Agent concludes that Georgia and North Carolina change-order scope talk did not leak into the Q1 accrual governed by the memo taxable-state list (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks whether GA/NC engagement change-order discussion affected a quarter that should follow the memo list only.
evidence: Per OE change-order search: Hannah Q1 correction excludes GA and NC; JE justification excludes GA and NC; C005 change-order kickoff 1df2c428fa125f8e9987639e2a036a2c is May 2026 onward (FP-2026-05) engagement scope. Check the Agent's final response to Ming.

title: The Agent sends an email to hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com with alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com copied.
justification: The prompt requires emailing Hannah the conclusion with Alex copied.
evidence: Per OE write step: email_send_email with sender ming.chang@brookfieldcpas.com, recipients hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com, cc alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com. Check the trajectory for email_send_email.

title: The Agent's email to hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com names $42,180.55 as the governing sign-off amount, per the posted closed-period adjusting entry that governs (not the channel/receipts-supported total of $40,364.55) (or similar).
justification: The prompt asks to email Hannah the conclusion after resolving which number governs; the sign-off amount is one distinct content requirement in that email, and the prompt anchors that figure to the posted closed-period entry.
evidence: Per OE email write: email_send_email content must state posted GL sign-off $42,180.55 as the governing figure per the closed-period anchor, not the channel/receipts-supported total. Check email_send_email content parameter.

title: The Agent reviews Hannah Grant's Q1 SaaS-taxability jurisdiction memo (doc_84905e8e14ad4d96) from Records Vault before drawing the partner-clearance conclusion.
justification: The prompt explicitly instructs the partner clearance to 'walk the support trail from Hannah's memo' to the posted entry. The memo is the authoritative SaaS-taxability jurisdiction list and is required by every valid solution path. A stricter Outcome rubric cannot capture this read step: an agent could in principle name the four taxable jurisdictions from the Slack thread alone without confirming them against the memo.
evidence: Check the trajectory for review of the jurisdiction memo (doc_84905e8e14ad4d96, Acme Cloud SaaS Multi-State Sales Tax Determination Memo Q1 2026) before the final response to Ming.

title: The Agent reviews Tom Chang's Q1 multi-state filing-receipts packet (doc_3e28a0ac0f464b56) from Records Vault before drawing the partner-clearance conclusion.
justification: The prompt explicitly instructs the partner clearance to walk the support trail through 'Tom's receipts to the posted FP-2026-03 liability'. The receipts packet is the documentary tie to the posted journal entry and is required by every valid solution path. A stricter Outcome rubric cannot capture this read step: an agent could in principle name the channel total from Slack alone without confirming it against the receipts packet.
evidence: Check the trajectory for review of the filing-receipts packet (doc_3e28a0ac0f464b56, Acme Cloud Q1 2026 Multi-State Sales Tax Filing Receipts Packet) before the final response to Ming.
